title: guajava montana
intro: Guajava montana, also known as the mountain guava or Chilean guava, is a shrub native to the Andes mountains in South America, characterized by its small, yellowish-green flowers and red, edible fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Guajava montana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Guajava montana can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and fruits can also be used in traditional medicine to create topical creams and ointments.
